description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a polished wooden backplate with a rich brown finish. The upper section houses two metallic bells and a central mouthpiece horn, both rendered in a muted metallic gray. Below the mouthpiece is a classic rotary dial with visible finger holes and numbers, encased in a circular metallic frame. A black cylindrical handset with metallic accents is attached to the left side of the base via a coiled cord, and rests in a dedicated cradle. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, combining wood, metal, and plastic elements for both function and aesthetic appeal. The object is compact, with a robust build and clear separation of interactive components.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden backplate, metallic bells, rotary dial, and a black handset attached by a coiled cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, mouthpiece, metallic bells, and the handset resting in its cradle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the wooden backplate and the protruding mouthpiece horn.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth wooden backplate and the coiled cord of the handset.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset in its cradle and the coiled cord attachment.
